data.narrative:
Astroscale, a publicly listed Space & Aerospace company founded by Mitsunobu Okada, raised $400M+ and currently trades at a $900M valuation. The peer universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, including Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Peer-median analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using three same-sector, same-stage public comparables—Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B)—the median valuation is $7.0B, with a range spanning $6.3B to $37.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; the sector median P/S is 42.9x across six tickers, but Astroscale's revenue is not on file, precluding this method.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $6.0B to $8.0B (median $7.0B), representing a 6.7x to 8.9x premium to the current $900M valuation. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set—Rocket Lab trades at a substantial premium, likely reflecting its advanced development and revenue stage—and the absence of revenue-normalized metrics. The median estimate assumes Astroscale's competitive positioning and market readiness align with Planet Labs and Firefly rather than Rocket Lab's premium valuation.
